Do not build the complete magazine, retire Aegis legacy scaffolding, or merge without explicit human approval.

tasks.title: Create Modern Blog Mockup Page with Cutting-Edge 2024-2025 Design Trends
tasks.description: Build an experimental blog mockup page at /app/mockup showcasing advanced design patterns including bento grid layouts, extreme typography contrasts, contemporary color systems, and sophisticated micro-interactions as a design laboratory for production implementation.
tasks.details:
Create a comprehensive mockup page implementing cutting-edge 2024-2025 web design trends:

BENTO GRID LAYOUT SYSTEM:
- Implement CSS Grid with asymmetrical layout using grid-template-areas
- Create responsive grid system with varying aspect ratios (1:1, 2:1, 1:2, 3:2)
- Use CSS Container Queries for dynamic grid adjustments
- Implement smooth transitions with transform: scale(1.02) and cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1) easing
- Add grid-auto-flow: dense for optimal space utilization

ADVANCED TYPOGRAPHY SYSTEM:
- Configure variable fonts: Playfair Display (400-900 weight range) for headlines, Inter (300-700) for body text
- Implement fluid typography using clamp() functions: clamp(120px, 12vw, 180px) for hero headlines
- Create kinetic typography with Intersection Observer API for scroll-triggered animations
- Use CSS @supports for progressive enhancement of variable font features
- Implement word-by-word reveal animations with staggered delays

CONTEMPORARY COLOR SYSTEM:
- Define Mocha Mousse (#A0826D) as primary with CSS custom properties
- Create dopamine accent palette: electric blue (#00D4FF), hot pink (#FF006B), vibrant yellow (#FFD700)
- Implement mesh gradients using CSS conic-gradient and radial-gradient combinations
- Configure true black (#000000) dark mode with prefers-color-scheme media queries
- Add dynamic color transitions with CSS color-mix() function

MICRO-INTERACTIONS & MOTION DESIGN:
- Implement magnetic cursor effects using mouse position tracking and transform: translate()
- Create parallax hero with transform3d for hardware acceleration
- Use Intersection Observer for fade-in animations with opacity and translateY transforms
- Add skeleton screens during theme transitions using CSS @keyframes shimmer effect
- Implement progress indicators with CSS custom properties for dynamic width updates

MODERN UI COMPONENTS:
- Create glassmorphism navigation with backdrop-filter: blur(10px) and rgba backgrounds
- Implement intelligent navbar hide/show using scroll direction detection
- Design neumorphic elements with multiple box-shadows for depth
- Create floating action buttons with position: fixed and z-index layering
- Build modern form inputs with floating labels using CSS :focus-within pseudo-class

CONTENT ORGANIZATION:
- Design full-width hero section with CSS object-fit: cover for imagery
- Create mixed grid layout with CSS Grid areas for feature boxes and post previews
- Implement horizontal scrolling category pills with scroll-snap-type: x mandatory
- Add animated author avatars using CSS transforms and hover states
- Build dynamic table of contents with smooth scrolling and active section highlighting

UNIQUE FEATURES:
- Implement thin reading progress bar using scroll position calculation and CSS width updates
- Create dynamic reading time estimation based on word count and average reading speed
- Build reaction system with CSS animations and local storage persistence
- Add share functionality with Clipboard API and toast notifications
- Implement command palette (⌘K) with fuzzy search using Fuse.js library
- Create recent searches with localStorage and search suggestions

PERFORMANCE & ACCESSIBILITY:
- Implement lazy loading with Intersection Observer and blur-up placeholder technique
- Use next/image with priority loading for above-the-fold content
- Add prefers-reduced-motion media queries to disable animations when requested
- Ensure keyboard navigation with visible focus states using :focus-visible
- Implement ARIA labels and semantic HTML structure
- Target Lighthouse scores: Performance 95+, Accessibility 100, Best Practices 95+, SEO 95+

TECHNICAL IMPLEMENTATION:
- Create modular component structure in packages/web/src/app/mockup/
- Use TypeScript interfaces for all props and data structures
- Implement custom hooks for scroll detection, theme switching, and local storage
- Create reusable animation utilities with Tailwind CSS classes
- Set up Storybook stories for all components
- Use CSS Modules or styled-components for component-scoped styles
tasks.testStrategy:
VISUAL TESTING:
- Verify bento grid layout responsiveness across breakpoints (320px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px)
- Test typography scaling and readability at different viewport sizes
- Validate color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA standards (4.5:1 minimum)
- Check glassmorphism effects render correctly across different browsers

INTERACTION TESTING:
- Test all micro-interactions (hover states, magnetic cursor, parallax scrolling)
- Verify smooth transitions and animations perform at 60fps
- Test command palette functionality with keyboard shortcuts (⌘K/Ctrl+K)
- Validate form inputs with floating labels and error states
- Test share functionality and clipboard API integration

PERFORMANCE TESTING:
- Run Lighthouse audits targeting 95+ scores across all metrics
- Measure First Contentful Paint <1.5s and Time to Interactive <3.5s
- Test lazy loading implementation with network throttling
- Verify Cumulative Layout Shift <0.1 with dynamic content loading
- Test bundle size optimization and code splitting effectiveness

ACCESSIBILITY TESTING:
- Test keyboard navigation through all interactive elements
- Verify screen reader compatibility with semantic HTML structure
- Test high contrast mode and reduced motion preferences
- Validate ARIA labels and roles for complex UI components
- Test voice navigation compatibility

CROSS-BROWSER TESTING:
- Test in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge latest versions
- Verify CSS Grid and Container Queries support with fallbacks
- Test variable font rendering and fallback font loading
- Validate backdrop-filter support with graceful degradation

RESPONSIVE TESTING:
- Test on mobile devices (iPhone, Android) for touch interactions
- Verify horizontal scrolling components work on touch devices
- Test grid layout adaptation on tablet and desktop viewports
- Validate typography scaling maintains readability across all devices

tasks.title: Implement Total Workflow Excellence System (TWES) for AI-Assisted Development
tasks.description: Deploy a layered context injection documentation system that supercharges AI-assisted development by providing both detailed prompts and reference documentation for each tool. The system includes automatic feedback loops where every error becomes a prevention rule, every success becomes a documented pattern, and every delay becomes an optimization target. Documentation freshness indicators ensure content stays current, while context inheritance prevents duplication and enables rapid protocol creation.
tasks.details:
Create and organize the following documentation structure to support the Total Workflow Excellence System (TWES):

1. **Tool-Specific Documentation Folders:**
   - Establish directories for each tool (e.g., `for-claude-bridge/`, `for-taskmaster/`, `for-agent/`).
   - Populate each with relevant prompts, usage examples, and tool-specific guidelines.

2. **Shared Context Documents:**
   - Maintain central documents for themes, file structure, performance standards, and design philosophy.
   - Ensure these are referenced and inherited by tool-specific documentation to avoid duplication.

3. **Context Bundles for Instant Loading:**
   - Develop scripts or automation to bundle and inject context for each tool, reducing setup time from 5-10 minutes to under 30 seconds.
   - Implement optimization techniques to ensure rapid context loading.

4. **Step-by-Step Protocols with Validation:**
   - Write detailed protocols for common workflows, including validation steps to ensure correctness.
   - Include error recovery procedures to minimize troubleshooting time.

5. **Troubleshooting Matrices and Error Prevention:**
   - Create matrices for common errors, their causes, and solutions.
   - Integrate error prevention tips into documentation and protocols.
   - Implement automatic feedback loops to convert errors into prevention rules.

6. **Context Inheritance:**
   - Implement a system where documentation inherits from shared context, ensuring DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principles.
   - Use inheritance to enable rapid protocol creation and updates.

7. **Living Metrics Dashboards:**
   - Set up dashboards to track documentation usage, protocol effectiveness, and productivity gains.
   - Monitor success rates, context gathering time, and error recovery metrics.

8. **Meta-Protocols for Creating New Protocols:**
   - Document how to create and update new protocols, ensuring the system evolves with use.
   - Include mechanisms for automatically contributing improvements back to the system.

9. **Session Continuity Docs:**
   - Maintain logs or docs for ongoing sessions, allowing users and AI to pick up where they left off.
   - Ensure seamless transitions between work sessions.

10. **Central Reference (CLAUDE.md):**
    - Create a master document (`CLAUDE.md`) that references all documentation, protocols, and dashboards, ensuring everyone (humans and AI) knows where everything is and how to use it.
    - Include visual system map, quick-start guide, and tool compatibility matrix.
    - Optimize for both human and AI consumption.

**Technical Approach:**
- Use Markdown for documentation and protocols for readability and version control.
- Automate context bundling using scripts or CI/CD pipelines.
- Integrate dashboards using lightweight analytics tools or custom-built solutions.
- Ensure all documentation is accessible and searchable within the project repository.
- Implement documentation freshness indicators to ensure content stays current.

**Considerations:**
- Align documentation with existing design systems and workflows (e.g., warm minimalism, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Next.js App Router).
- Validate that all tool-specific documentation is up-to-date and references the latest shared context.
- Monitor and iterate on the system based on usage metrics and feedback.

**Success Criteria:**
- Any developer (human or AI) can complete any documented task with >90% first-attempt success rate
- Developers spend <10% of their time on context gathering
- System enables automatic contribution of improvements
- Context load time <30 seconds
- Error recovery time <2 minutes
- New developer productive within 2 hours
- AI tool success rate >95%

**Implementation Phases:**
- Phase 1 (Foundation): Core structure and reference
- Phase 2 (Core Systems): Protocols and inheritance
- Phase 3 (Intelligence): Metrics and continuity
- Phase 4 (Optimization): Automation and improvement
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Documentation Structure Validation:**
   - Verify that all tool-specific and shared documentation folders exist and are correctly organized.
   - Check that `CLAUDE.md` references all documentation and protocols.
   - Validate that the visual system map, quick-start guide, and tool compatibility matrix are complete and accurate.

2. **Context Injection Testing:**
   - Test context bundling scripts to ensure they load required documentation in under 30 seconds.
   - Validate that context inheritance works as expected, with no duplication.
   - Measure actual load times across different environments and tools.

3. **Protocol and Workflow Testing:**
   - Execute step-by-step protocols for common workflows, ensuring each step is clear and validation checks pass.
   - Confirm that troubleshooting matrices are accessible and provide actionable solutions.
   - Test error recovery procedures and verify they resolve issues within the 2-minute target.

4. **Dashboard and Metrics Verification:**
   - Check that living metrics dashboards display up-to-date usage and productivity data.
   - Validate that dashboards are accessible to all relevant stakeholders.
   - Verify metrics accurately track success rates, context gathering time, and other KPIs.

5. **Session Continuity Check:**
   - Simulate interrupted sessions and verify that session continuity docs allow users and AI to resume work accurately.
   - Test transitions between different developers working on the same task.

6. **Meta-Protocol Validation:**
   - Follow meta-protocols to create a new protocol and ensure the process is documented and repeatable.
   - Test automatic contribution mechanisms for system improvements.

7. **Cross-Referencing and Accessibility:**
   - Ensure all documentation is cross-referenced and accessible within the project repository.
   - Confirm that both humans and AI can navigate and use the system effectively.

8. **New Developer Onboarding Test:**
   - Have a new team member use the system to become productive within the 2-hour target.
   - Measure their success rate and productivity compared to established benchmarks.

9. **AI Tool Integration Testing:**
   - Verify AI tools can successfully utilize the documentation system with >95% success rate.
   - Test across different AI tools and use cases to ensure consistent performance.

10. **Phase Completion Validation:**
    - Establish clear criteria for each implementation phase.
    - Verify all requirements for each phase are met before proceeding to the next.

subtasks.testStrategy: Test with actual new team members to verify they become productive within 2 hours. Collect feedback on the onboarding experience and iterate to improve effectiveness. Measure actual time to productivity against the 2-hour target.

subtasks.title: Create Team Training & Adoption Program
subtasks.description: Develop comprehensive training materials and adoption strategies for TWES
subtasks.details: Develop onboarding guides specifically for TWES usage. Create video walkthroughs demonstrating common workflows and best practices. Establish a 'TWES Champions' program to identify and train internal advocates. Design workshops for different skill levels. Create quick reference cards for common tasks. Develop a certification program to ensure proficiency. Build a feedback mechanism to continuously improve training materials based on user experience.

subtasks.title: Implement Feedback Loop Automation
subtasks.description: Build automated systems to capture and process improvement opportunities
subtasks.details: Develop scripts to auto-capture error patterns from git commits and logs. Create algorithms to identify recurring issues and generate improvement suggestions. Build automation to convert common errors into prevention rules automatically. Implement pattern recognition for successful workflows to document best practices. Create weekly 'system health' reports showing documentation effectiveness, error trends, and improvement opportunities. Design the system to proactively suggest documentation updates based on usage patterns.

subtasks.title: Develop Emergency Recovery Protocols
subtasks.description: Create fallback procedures to maintain productivity when tools are unavailable
subtasks.details: Document manual workarounds for each AI tool in case of outages. Create offline versions of critical documentation for access during network issues. Develop context recovery procedures for partial or corrupted sessions. Design fallback protocols for common workflows that can be executed without AI assistance. Create emergency checklists for rapid problem diagnosis. Implement backup systems for critical documentation access. Ensure all recovery procedures maintain productivity levels as close to normal as possible.

subtasks.title: Create Principle-Based Documentation System
subtasks.description: Develop comprehensive principle-based documentation that teaches AI and developers HOW to think about features rather than prescribing specific implementations
subtasks.details:
Created 5 foundational principle documents that provide frameworks for evaluating and implementing ANY feature:

1. **Design Implementation Principles** - Framework for evaluating any visual feature against mission, user impact, and technical constraints
2. **Layout System Guidelines** - Principles for building ANY layout system with focus on clarity, adaptability, accessibility, performance, and consistency
3. **Animation & Motion Principles** - When and how to use motion meaningfully, with performance budgets and accessibility considerations
4. **Progressive Enhancement Strategy** - How to layer ANY feature based on user capabilities, network conditions, and device constraints
5. **Feature Evaluation Framework** - Meta-framework that ties all principles together with a 5-gate evaluation process

These documents teach systematic thinking applicable to any feature rather than prescribing specific implementations. They enable AI and developers to make principled decisions autonomously.
